activities_projects.title: Adiabatic Temperature Lapse Rate Model
activities_projects.objective: Calculate the temperature drop as air rises through the Troposphere
steps: Research the 'Normal Lapse Rate' (approx. 6.5°C per 1000m)
Calculate the hypothetical temperature at the peak of Mt. Everest if the base temperature is 30°C
Plot a graph showing the inverse relationship between Altitude and Temperature in the Troposphere
Explain why the temperature trend reverses in the Stratosphere
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the vertical thermal structure of the atmosphere and its mathematical decline
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the temperature calculations and graph interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Coriolis Force and Wind Deflection
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate why winds do not move in a straight line
steps: Attempt to draw a straight line from the North Pole to the Equator on a stationary globe
Spin the globe slowly and try to draw the same straight line
Observe the curve (deflection) created by the rotation
Apply 'Ferrel’s Law' to explain why winds deflect to the right in the Northern Hemisphere
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the impact of Earth's rotation on planetary wind directions
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct demonstration of deflection and application of Ferrel’s Law (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Cyclonic vs. Anticyclonic Pressure Gradients
activities_projects.objective: Analyze isobaric patterns in low and high-pressure systems
steps: Identify 'Isobars' (lines of equal pressure) on a meteorological chart
Determine the 'Pressure Gradient' by measuring the distance between isobars
Map the inward-spiraling wind of a Cyclone vs. the outward-spiraling wind of an Anticyclone
Explain why closer isobars indicate higher wind speeds
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes the relationship between air pressure intensity and wind velocity
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy of the wind flow diagrams and gradient analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Hygroscopic Nuclei and Rain Initiation
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the role of dust and smoke in cloud formation
steps: Add warm water to the bottle and seal it; observe that no cloud forms despite pressure changes
Drop a lit match inside to provide 'smoke particles' (Hygroscopic nuclei)
Squeeze and release the bottle rapidly
Observe the sudden appearance of a thick cloud only when nuclei are present
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that moisture requires physical particles to condense into clouds
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific explanation of why smoke was necessary for cloud visibility (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Global Warming & Carbon Footprint Calculus
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the impact of Greenhouse Gases ($CO_2$) on the atmosphere
steps: Calculate the $CO_2$ emissions of your household based on monthly energy units
Research the 'Global Warming Potential' (GWP) of Methane vs. $CO_2$
Discuss the 'Albedo Effect'—how melting ice in the Polar regions leads to further warming
Formulate a strategy to reduce the atmospheric carbon load of your school
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies mathematical modeling to environmental conservation and the Greenhouse Effect
activities_projects.evaluation: Precision of the footprint calculation and logic of the reduction strategy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Orographic Rainfall & Rain Shadow Quantitative Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the moisture disparity between windward and leeward sides
materials: Rainfall data for Mumbai vs. Pune
Physical map of the Western Ghats
steps: Plot the annual rainfall of a coastal city (Windward) vs. an inland city (Leeward) behind a mountain
Explain the 'Rain Shadow' effect in terms of descending dry air and warming
Calculate the percentage difference in precipitation between the two zones
Discuss the vegetation changes observed in these two areas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how topography alters atmospheric moisture distribution
activities_projects.evaluation: Statistical comparison of rainfall and topographical reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Ionosphere and Long-Range Communication
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the role of the Thermosphere in radio transmission
steps: Research why AM radio signals travel further at night
Diagram how the 'Ionosphere' reflects radio waves back to Earth
Explain the impact of Solar Flares on satellite communication in the Exosphere
Describe the molecular state of air in the Thermosphere where temperatures reach 1,500°C
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the chemical and electrical properties of upper layers to modern technology
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the radio-wave reflection diagram and technical explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Frontal Systems Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Study the interaction between different air masses
steps: Fill one side of the tank with cold blue water and the other with hot red water
Slowly remove the divider
Observe how the cold air mass (blue) slides under the warm air mass (red)
Identify the 'Front' and predict the type of weather it would produce
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the formation of Cyclonic (Frontal) rainfall and air mass boundaries
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed observation of the water interface and front identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Anemometer Calibration and Beaufort Scale
activities_projects.objective: Convert physical rotations into standardized wind speed units
materials: DIY Anemometer (4 cups on a spindle)
Moving vehicle (with adult help) or a fan
steps: Count the Rotations Per Minute (RPM) at a known speed (e.g., 20 km/h)
Create a conversion table for RPM to km/h
Observe local wind effects (leaves moving vs. branches breaking)
Classify the wind using the 'Beaufort Scale' from 0 to 12
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Standardizes observational data into scientific meteorological units
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the calibration table and Beaufort classification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Atmosphere: Air Quality Index (AQI) Project
activities_projects.objective: Analyze particulate matter (PM) concentrations in different zones
steps: Place 'Soot Traps' (slides) in a residential area, a park, and a busy traffic junction for 24 hours
Observe the density of particles under a magnifying glass
Research the health impacts of $PM_{2.5}$ and $PM_{10}$
Propose an urban planning layout to improve air circulation and reduce AQI
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the human-made (Anthropogenic) changes to air composition
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative slide analysis and scientific urban planning proposal (10 marks)
